title: erythroxylum boivinianum
intro: Erythroxylum boivinianum, also known as Boivin's coca, is a species of shrub in the coca family (Erythroxylaceae) native to Madagascar. The medicinal properties of Erythroxylum boivinianum include its ability to act as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as fever, headache, and muscle pain. The health benefits of using Erythroxylum boivinianum include its potential to boost energy levels, improve mental clarity, and reduce stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, Erythroxylum boivinianum is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
